item.title: Yuan to Dodge Seasonal Slump on Iran War Resilience, Economy
item.description: China’s yuan is poised to defy its typical second-quarter slump as the nation’s resilience to the Iran war and a burgeoning economic recovery offset seasonal pressures.

item.title: Samsung Profit Up Eight-Fold After AI Chip Sales Defy War Fears
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. earned a stronger-than-expected eight-fold leap in quarterly profit, underscoring robust demand for memory chips used for artificial intelligence and data centers even amid uncertainties triggered by war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Holds Gains With US-Iran Deadline in Focus: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity futures were steady and oil held onto gains as President Donald Trump signaled a potential escalation of strikes on Iran as soon as Tuesday, overshadowing hopes for a ceasefire.

item.title: Trump Threatens to Hit Civilian Targets If Iran Doesn’t Open Hormuz
item.description: On today’s Big Take podcast: Unpacking President Trump’s mixed rhetoric on Iran, the status of ceasefire talks and why he wants to collect tolls for the strait.

item.title: OpenAI Calls for Investigation Into Musk by California, Delaware
item.description: OpenAI urged the attorneys general of California and Delaware to investigate potential “improper and anti-competitive behavior” by Elon Musk in his efforts to block OpenAI from restructuring as a for-profit company.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Trump’s Deadline to Strike Iran
item.description: Gold steadied after a two-day decline as traders weighed US President Donald Trump’s latest threat to destroy Iranian infrastructure if an agreement isn’t reached before a Tuesday deadline.

item.title: Oil Holds Near Highest Since 2022 as Trump Threats Escalate
item.description: US oil held near its highest close since June 2022 as President Donald Trump escalated threats to obliterate key Iranian infrastructure if his terms aren’t met before a Tuesday deadline.

item.title: One-Dyas Expands Gas Output in Protected North Sea Marine Zone
item.description: One-Dyas BV has started production from a second well at its platform in the North Sea, lifting annual output at the site to about 1 billion cubic meters of gas.

item.title: Two Sigma Profits From Chaotic March, Beating Multistrat Peers
item.description: Two Sigma Investments’ biggest hedge funds generated standout returns during a frantic month for markets, edging out peers even as the firm grapples with executive infighting.

item.title: Anthropic Tops $30 Billion Run Rate, Seals Deal With Broadcom
item.description: Anthropic PBC said its revenue run rate has now topped $30 billion, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025, and confirmed plans to work with Broadcom Inc. and Google to power its burgeoning operations.

item.title: Brazil Expands Fuel Tax Cuts, Subsidies as Iran War Carries On
item.description: Brazil’s government will expand federal tax cuts and subsidies on fuels as it attempts to shield consumers from rising prices due to the war in Iran, broadening measures it had previously unveiled amid the conflict.

item.title: Phillips 66 Sees Nearly $1 Billion in Losses as Oil Prices Surge
item.description: Phillips 66 estimated nearly $1 billion in losses from its short position in oil and other related commodity derivative contracts in the first quarter as the war in Iran sent crude and fuel prices skyrocketing.

item.title: Goldman Says It’s Ready to Pounce as Retail Flees Private Credit
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. says a reliance on stickier, more patient institutional investors, rather than wealthy individuals, helped its $15.7 billion private credit fund narrowly escape the broader exodus plaguing peers this year.

item.title: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Unite to Combat Model Copying in China
item.description: Rivals OpenAI, Anthropic PBC, and Alphabet Inc.’s Google have begun working together to try to clamp down on Chinese competitors extracting results from cutting-edge US artificial intelligence models to gain an edge in the global AI race.

item.title: Underwater Mortgages Force China’s Banks to Get More Creative
item.description: Lenders are trying to prevent defaults and foreclosures that risk deepening the country’s housing crisis.

item.title: Is SpaceX Worth $2 Trillion? Key Questions for Musk’s Big IPO
item.description: In the seven and a half years since Apple Inc.’s market capitalization broke through the $1 trillion mark, investors have become accustomed to seeing big tech companies command 13-digit valuations in public markets.

item.title: Amazon Reaches US Postal Service Deal for 1 Billion Packages
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. has reached a new agreement with the US Postal Service, providing a lifeline to the beleaguered government agency and securing delivery for customers in rural America.

item.title: Nvidia-Backed Data Center Builder Firmus Raises $505 Million
item.description: Data center builder Firmus Technologies Pty raised $505 million in an investment round led by Coatue Management LLC, part of a global push to finance artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Bitcoin Tops $70,000 as Traders Unwind Bets Amid War Tensions
item.description: Bitcoin climbed above $70,000 on Monday for the first time since March, as traders rushed to pull back bearish positions despite the lack of clarity on ceasefire negotiations in the Middle East.

item.title: MAK Capital Tells Evotec to List US Unit Worth Over €1 billion
item.description: Activist fund MAK Capital, led by Michael A. Kaufman, has urged the leadership of German biotech firm Evotec SE to list its US unit and speed up its cost cutting program after the shares have lost about 85% over the past five years.

item.title: Retail Crowd Seen Resuming Stock-Buying Binge After Tax Deadline
item.description: Equity bulls expecting a bumpy ride for US equities to be over soon have a historically favorable seasonal pattern on their side.

item.title: SpaceX’s IPO Pitch Centers on Elon Musk’s Ability to ‘Sell the Dream’
item.description: SpaceX and investment bankers will host meetings to pressure-test the Elon Musk-led company’s targeted $2 trillion valuation, a crucial step as it drives toward the largest-ever initial public offering, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Apollo-Backed Yahoo Kicks Off Talks on $1.6 Billion Refinancing
item.description: Yahoo Inc. has started discussions with lenders to refinance about $1.6 billion of leveraged loans, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Chatter About Fed’s Balance Sheet Is Ahead of Reality, CIBC Says
item.description: Fixed‑income markets are overestimating the impact of potential changes to the Federal Reserve’s balance‑sheet policy, which are likely to be slow and limited, according to Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Plans Private Credit Fund Even as Investors Flee
item.description: Morgan Stanley plans to debut an interval fund investing predominantly in private credit, just as retail investment vehicles in the $1.8 trillion market are being hit with record redemption requests.

item.title: Yardeni Sees Tech Offering Attractive Entry Point After Pullback
item.description: Technology stocks have returned to attractive levels for investors willing to play the long game after a pullback from last year’s record highs, according to veteran strategist Ed Yardeni.

item.title: Japan M&A Momentum to Persist, Alvarez & Marsal’s Aversano Says
item.description: Deal flow in Japan will likely keep up its brisk pace, thanks in part to regulatory policies that are fueling mergers and acquisitions as well as broader macroeconomic trends, according to Alvarez & Marsal Inc.’s Paul Aversano.

item.title: US Crude Exports Test Limits as Shipping Constraints Mount
item.description: The war in Iran has sent demand for US oil from overseas toward record levels, bolstering President Donald Trump’s push for “American energy dominance.” But as shipments surge, exports are beginning to run up against their practical limits.

item.title: Jones Day Law Firm Says Hackers Accessed Some Clients’ Data
item.description: Hackers have breached Jones Day and accessed the files of 10 clients, the law firm said on Monday.

item.title: US Truck Rates at Highest Since 2022 Add to Inflation Pressures
item.description: Skyrocketing fuel prices due to the Iran war are fanning the embers of transportation costs, which were already rising due to a shrinking pool of drivers in the US.
